title: racosperma quornense
intro: Racosperma quornense, also known as Acacia quornensis, is a species of shrub in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to promote healing and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, Racosperma quornense is often used to make salves, ointments, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations, burns, and minor wounds.
